marketState: neutral-to-constructive medium-term, bearish-tilted near-term — weather is the wildcard
summary: Front-month NG sits almost exactly at fair value ($2.909 vs $2.919 model fair), so there is no mean-reversion edge from price-vs-fair today, but the fair-value curve itself climbs to $3.035 by +21 days. The standout signal is the 8-16 day weather window, running +46% above the 5-year norm and above the historical max range — a significant late-summer cooling-demand catalyst the tape has not caught up to. Positioning adds a contrarian tailwind: managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of the past 5 years, a crowded short vulnerable to squeeze if that heat verifies. Working against the bulls: storage is +183 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish build surprise last week, and 5 of 6 technical timeframes are bearish-trending with negative MACD, so near-term price action still reflects the storage overhang rather than the incoming heat. Net: cautiously constructive on a 3-12 week view, mixed-to-cautious on a 1-10 day view.

longTerm.sizing: 0.75R initial (approx 0.75% account risk); size down vs 1R given Low seasonality reliability and storage surplus headwind
longTerm.rationale: Price is -5.0pp vs seasonal median and YTD running well below the typical-year path, while 8-16D weather is +46% vs 5y and above the max range — a heat-driven demand catalyst not yet reflected in a fair-value curve that itself rises to $3.035 by +21D. Managed-money net-short at the 8th 5y percentile adds contrarian upside risk (squeeze potential) heading into seasonally strong September.
longTerm.invalidation: 8-16D heat anomaly fades back toward normal in updated forecasts, storage surprises continue printing bearish, or price closes convincingly below $2.60

shortTerm.sizing: 0.5R (approx 0.5% account risk); reduced size given crowded short COT positioning raises squeeze risk
shortTerm.rationale: 5 of 6 technical timeframes (5m/1h/4h/1d/1w) show bearish trend with 50<200 EMA cross and negative MACD, and last week's storage build beat forecast by +17 Bcf (bearish surprise) — near-term momentum and fundamentals both point down while price offers no cheap entry since it sits right at fair value.
shortTerm.invalidation: 1h reclaims the lower Bollinger band with RSI turning up through 45+, or price breaks and holds above $3.05, especially if 1-7D weather data starts pricing in the 8-16D heat spike
